experiment: Cluster C — HYBRID verifier (core-claim direction + authoritative oracles), LIVE
authoritative_coverage: 0/43 contaminated cases — Google + Wikidata/Crossref cover NONE of this pack's 'fabricated study attributes X to Y' claims (not viral myths, not simple authorship facts).
finding: The hybrid (core-claim DIRECTION fed by AUTHORITATIVE oracles) achieves 0% over-block as designed, but on THIS pack the authoritative oracles have ZERO coverage (0/43), so authoritative-only catches 0% and the hybrid's catch comes entirely from the flagged LOW-independence LLM tail (58.1%, == core-on-openworld). The hoped-for 'high catch AND low over-block' did NOT materialize here: it is bounded by whether an independent oracle actually covers the contamination domain. NO FREE LUNCH — catching contamination needs EITHER an oracle that covers the claim (sparse), OR fail-closed strictness (over-blocks), OR model knowledge (low independence). This pack's provenance-fabrications ('a fabricated 2023 study attributes...') are an existence-check problem (does the cited study exist in Crossref?), NOT a claim-rating problem — so a CITATION-EXISTENCE verifier (the repo's legal_citation_exists / crossref doi_resolver pattern) is the right high-independence tool for this domain, not a rating oracle.
